title: Elaeoselinum foetidum
taxonomy: Elaeoselinum foetidum belongs to the family Apiaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. Elaeoselinum foetidum is closely related to other plants in the same family, such as carrots and parsley. The plant's classification is further divided into several species, with Elaeoselinum foetidum being one of them. More research is needed to confirm its exact classification within the Apiaceae family.
uses: Elaeoselinum foetidum, also known as Indian pennywort, is a plant with various medicinal uses. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and digestive problems. intro: Elaeoselinum foetidum, also known as wild fennel or bitter fennel, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to aid digestion, relieve flatulence, and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The bioactive constituents of Elaeoselinum foetidum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its health benefits.
